sections.title: Overview
sections.content: The Zendesk Sell connector syncs contacts, leads, deals, notes, tasks, and pipelines from Zendesk Sell (formerly Base CRM) into the Shielva knowledge base. It uses the Zendesk Sell REST API v3 with OAuth 2.0 Authorization Code flow. The connector implements page-based pagination (page + per_page) with meta.links.next_page detection, exponential backoff retry with jitter, and stable SHA-256 source IDs for idempotent ingestion.

subsections.title: OAuth 2.0 Authorization Code
subsections.content: Zendesk Sell uses OAuth 2.0 Authorization Code flow. You register an OAuth application in Zendesk Sell Settings → OAuth → Developer Apps to obtain a Client ID and Client Secret. The user is redirected to the Zendesk Sell consent page and, after approval, an authorization code is exchanged for an access_token. All API requests use Authorization: Bearer {access_token}.
steps: Log into Zendesk Sell and go to Settings → OAuth → Developer Apps.
Click Register Application and fill in your Application Name and Redirect URI.
Copy the Client ID and Client Secret shown after saving.
In Shielva ACP, enter the Client ID, Client Secret, and Redirect URI in the connector install fields.
Click Authorize — you will be redirected to Zendesk Sell to approve access.
After approval, Shielva exchanges the code for an access_token automatically.

fields.label: Client ID
fields.description: The OAuth 2.0 Client ID from your Zendesk Sell registered application.

fields.label: Client Secret
fields.description: The OAuth 2.0 Client Secret from your Zendesk Sell registered application.

fields.label: Redirect URI
fields.description: The URI Zendesk Sell redirects to after authorization. Must match the URI registered in your OAuth application.

items.name: Contacts
items.description: People and companies you have contact with. Includes name, email, phone, title, organization, and website.

items.name: Leads
items.description: Unqualified prospects. Includes name, email, phone, organization, title, and status.

items.name: Deals
items.description: Sales opportunities. Includes name, value, currency, status, stage, owner, contact, and organization.

items.name: Notes
items.description: Free-text notes attached to contacts, leads, or deals.

items.name: Tasks
items.description: Action items with due dates attached to CRM records.

items.name: Pipelines
items.description: Sales pipelines and their stage definitions.

sections.title: Pagination
sections.content: Zendesk Sell API v3 uses page-based pagination. The connector passes page and per_page (default 100) query parameters. When the response meta.links.next_page field is non-null, the connector fetches the next page by incrementing the page counter. Pagination continues until next_page is null or an empty items array is returned.

sections.title: Error Handling
sections.content: The connector maps HTTP status codes to typed exceptions: 401/403 → ZendeskSellAuthError (not retried), 404 → ZendeskSellNotFoundError, 429 → ZendeskSellRateLimitError (honours Retry-After header), 5xx → ZendeskSellServerError, network failures → ZendeskSellNetworkError. Transient errors (network, 5xx, rate limit) are retried up to 3 times with exponential backoff (base 1s, max 30s) plus random jitter.

sections.title: Stable Source IDs
sections.content: Every normalized document has a stable 16-character source_id computed as SHA-256('entity_type:id')[:16]. This ensures the same CRM record produces the same source_id across repeated syncs, enabling idempotent upsert into the Shielva knowledge base.

methods.name: install()
methods.description: Validates the access_token by calling GET /users/self. Returns InstallResult with ConnectorHealth and AuthStatus.

methods.name: authorize()
methods.description: Returns the OAuth 2.0 authorization URL for the user to approve access. Include redirect_uri in config to have it appended.

methods.name: health_check()
methods.description: Pings GET /users/self and returns HealthCheckResult. Healthy → 200 OK; Degraded → network issue; Offline → auth failure or missing token.

methods.name: sync(full, since, kb_id)
methods.description: Fetches all pages of contacts, leads, deals, notes, and tasks. Normalizes each record into a ConnectorDocument. If kb_id is provided, calls _ingest_document for each record.

methods.name: list_contacts(page, per_page)
methods.description: Returns a single page of contacts from GET /v3/contacts.

methods.name: list_leads(page, per_page)
methods.description: Returns a single page of leads from GET /v3/leads.

methods.name: list_deals(page, per_page)
methods.description: Returns a single page of deals from GET /v3/deals.

methods.name: list_notes(page, per_page)
methods.description: Returns a single page of notes from GET /v3/notes.

methods.name: list_tasks(page, per_page)
methods.description: Returns a single page of tasks from GET /v3/tasks.
